situation_brief: CSK and KKR face a critical 0-3 and 0-2 deficit with NRR below -1.900. The top four remain unbeaten, creating a massive 4-point gap that forces the bottom half into a must-win cycle.

elimination_watch.insight: With 0 points and a league-worst -2.467 NRR, CSK must win 72% of remaining games to reach the 16-point safety line. Their NRR is effectively a one-game penalty in tie-break scenarios.
Winless after two games with a -1.964 NRR, KKR needs a drastic turnaround. Their upcoming LSG and CSK fixtures are mandatory wins to avoid early mathematical irrelevance.
Two wins from two with the highest NRR in the league provides a massive cushion. They can afford three losses while remaining ahead of the mid-table pack on tie-breakers.
Zero points from two games is manageable, but their -0.424 NRR is better than other winless teams. A win against DC is vital to prevent falling two games behind the top four.

qualification_math.fact: Teams reaching 16 points have a 94% historical qualification rate, requiring 8 wins from the 14-game schedule.
CSK requires a 0.727 win percentage for the remainder of the season to reach the 16-point benchmark.
The 4.882 NRR spread between RCB and CSK creates a virtual 2-point advantage for the leaders in any points deadlock.
A win for RR tonight would make them the third team to reach 6 points, potentially creating a 6-point gulf between the top and bottom of the table.

scenarios.impact: RR moves to 6 points, joining PBKS at the top and pushing MI into a 1-2 record with negative NRR.
MI levels at 4 points with RR, DC, and RCB, creating a five-way congestion at the top of the table.
